---
title: Network Analysis Resources
---

## Courses

::: {.column-screen-inset}

<!-- prettier-ignore -->
| Course | Instructor | Institution | Platform | Note |
|--------|------------|-------------|----------|------|
| [CS224W: Machine Learning with Graphs (Fall 2023)](https://web.stanford.edu/class/cs224w/) | Jure Leskovec | Stanford University | NA | NA |
| [Social and Economic Networks: Models and Analysis](https://www.coursera.org/learn/social-economic-networks) | Matthew O. Jackson | Stanford University | Coursera | NA |
| [Methods for Network Analysis with R](https://www.bookdown.org/markhoff/social_network_analysis/) | Mark Hoff | Stanford University | Bookdown | NA |
| [NPTEL: Social Network Analysis](https://archive.nptel.ac.in/courses/106/106/106106239/) | Tanmoy Chakraborty | IIT Madras | NPTEL | NA |

:::

## Books

::: {.column-screen-inset}

<!-- prettier-ignore -->
| Book | Author | Publisher | Note |
|------|--------|-----------|------|
| [Graph Representation Learning](https://www.cs.mcgill.ca/~wlh/grl_book/) | William L. Hamilton | Springer | NA |
| [Networks, Crowds, and Markets: Reasoning About a Highly Connected World](https://www.cs.cornell.edu/home/kleinber/networks-book/) | David Easley, Jon Kleinberg | Cambridge University Press | NA |
| [Network Science](http://networksciencebook.com/) | Albert-László Barabási | Cambridge University Press | NA |

:::

## Papers

::: {.column-screen-inset}

Pass

:::

## Projects

::: {.column-screen-inset}

Pass

:::
